repo_prior_art.cite: pid_dose_response_shape_v1: finite-grid shape-closed outer-bound LP for continuous-treatment sensitivity intervals, but without IV, copula tube, or rank-slice slope

published_prior_art.cite: FirpoRidder2019 + optimal-transport/Makarov coupling logic; none gives this cIV+copula rank-slice slope LP

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_dose_response_shape_v1 is dosewise shape/sensitivity pasting without IV or rank-copula dependence; pid_dynamic_iv_compliance_v2 is binary dynamic compliance, not continuous-dose rank dependence

published_prior_art.cite: Kasy2014 gives continuous-IV marginal distributions; BaitairianSebastienJreichKatsahianGuilloux2025 and DalalTchetgenTchetgen2025 give continuous-treatment sensitivity bounds, not one-law cIV sensitivity plus adjacent-dose rank-copula sharpness

ChernozhukovHansen2005/2006 identify structural quantile effects under rank restrictions; they do not give partial-ID sign certification from bounded-confounding envelopes intersected with a copula tube

repo_prior_art.cite: none as a catalogue statement; closest pid_dose_response_shape_v1 has a no-hidden-confounding collapse, not a copula-irrelevance mean-slope check

published_prior_art.cite: FirpoRidder2019: ATE is the point-identified functional from marginal potential-outcome distributions; this theorem is the same linearity sanity check for the proposed slope object

structure_flags.label: Bibliography
structure_flags.one_line: MarmarelisHaddadJessonJahanshadGalstyanVerSteeg2023 is listed as AAAI, but verified publication venue is UAI 2023 / PMLR 216.

soundness_flags.label: Theorem 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The finite-grid LP sums cells with r_b in S_tau and divides by tau_U - tau_L, but Assumption grid never requires S_tau to be grid-aligned or the selected b-cells to have total mass tau_U - tau_L.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Add a grid-alignment convention for S_tau in Theorem 1, or replace the objective by cell-intersection weights so the denominator matches selected rank mass.
Include an explicit two-bin or three-bin numerical witness for Conjecture 2 showing positive rho<1/2 lower endpoint while rho=1 and full-population intervals include zero.
Add one direct local-structural-quantile comparator, such as Chesher-style local effects or recent IVQR rank-similarity partial-ID work, to make the rank-slope boundary sharper.
State the common-latent-law compatibility conditions needed in Conjecture 1 separately from the dosewise envelope definition, so failure modes are testable rather than hidden in R^{cIV+cop}.

recommended_next_step: Patch the grid-slice denominator and Marmarelis venue metadata, then add a concrete finite-rank phase-transition witness before Stage 0.
